market_context: Trading day (Mon) after the July-4 weekend; snapshot bars end 07-02. Tape: 'Dow Jones Hits Record High as Chip Bloodbath Sinks Nasdaq 100' (07-02), but flows may be turning - 'Tech Is Winning Wall Street's Cash: Tech Funds Draw $14.3 Billion in Second-Biggest Weekly Inflow on Record' (07-06) after 'Popular Magnificent 7 ETF Falls 8.7%' (07-05). VOO 684.68, +0.5% over 30d; 'SCHD ETF Is Beating VOO and QQQ This Year' (07-05). Data pull PARTIAL for the 3rd consecutive run: fundamentals failed for all 7 tickers (proxy 403), prices+news intact - sector caps cannot be enforced, so any buy would be rejected sector-unknown; per the 07-03 plan, the JNJ buy stays parked until a clean-data day. Portfolio is 100% cash, so the holdings-only breaking-news web sweep was not applicable. No thesis-grade catalyst crossed the bar today: all holds.

signals.thesis: Silicon supply story firming: 'Broadcom, Apple To Expand Collaboration Through 2031 To Develop, Supply Tailored ASIC Products For Apple' (07-06) locks in custom-chip capacity, while 'After Apple, Intel Raises Chip Prices by Up to $50' (07-06) shows input-cost inflation persisting. Price 306.80, +2.0% over 30d, mid-range. Neither settles the demand question from last week's price hikes. Falsified toward sell if hikes dent iPhone-cycle demand; toward buy if margins hold at launch.
sources: Benzinga 2026-07-06: Broadcom, Apple To Expand Collaboration Through 2031 To Develop, Supply Tailored ASIC Products For Apple
Benzinga 2026-07-06: After Apple, Intel Raises Chip Prices by Up to $50, Citing 'Market Dynamics'

signals.thesis: Mixed, not thesis-grade: 'Data Center Leases Top $850 Billion as Meta, Microsoft, Oracle Lead Record AI Infrastructure Spending' (07-05) confirms capex commitment, layoffs announced in the week wrap ('Consumer Tech (June 29-July 2): Microsoft Announces Layoffs', 07-05), and '$14.3 Billion' tech-fund inflows (07-06) may stabilize the -7.4% 30d drawdown. Price 390.30, lower third of range. Still missing Azure guidance or Copilot-merge confirmation; hold until one lands.

signals.thesis: Sentiment fight at the 30d low: bulls - Dan Niles calls the AI chip selloff a 'Speed Bump' as Vera Rubin fuels long-term demand (07-06) and tech funds drew $14.3B, second-biggest weekly inflow on record (07-06); bears - Michael Burry 'Rejects Claims He Was Wrong on Nvidia' (07-06). Price 194.51 vs 30d low 191.97, -13.0% over 30d. No fundamentals data to judge valuation support and no company-specific catalyst; not catching the knife, nothing held to sell. Re-evaluate if 191.97 breaks.

signals.thesis: Regulatory drumbeat, no resolution: 'Meta & Google Sued In Several States' (week wrap, 07-05) and 'US Tech Sector Pushes Back Against UK's Child Safety Social Media Regulations' (07-06) extend the 07-02 EU-fine overhang; today's only fresh mention is 'Final Trades' show chatter (07-06), which this process ignores. Price 359.07, -7.5% over 30d, off the 335.79 low but not stabilized. Hold; revisit on price stabilization with no new adverse rulings.

signals.thesis: June buy thesis (IMAAVY EAN data, TECVAYLI CHMP nod, Ebola vaccine optionality) intact, but today's only fresh headline is an earnings preview - 'Johnson & Johnson Likely To Report Higher Q2 Earnings' (07-06) - analyst chatter, not a catalyst. Price 263.04 is the exact 30d high, +14.7% over 30d, and fundamentals are again missing (sector-unknown rejection certain). Hold; re-propose buy on a clean-data day if close holds above 240, reassess at Q2 earnings.
